label: 618_fishery_noaa_fisheries_shark
description: Legislation aimed at banning the sale, transport, and possession of shark fins in the United States. The bills draw on existing fisheries management laws to enforce these bans while allowing for certain exceptions.

earliest_articles.title: H.R. 511, a bill to amend title 18, United States Code, to prohibit the importation of various injurious species of constrictor snakes
earliest_articles.excerpt: H.R. 511 would make it a federal crime to import or ship certain snakes into the United States; the bill would exempt state fish and wildlife agencies and exhibitors from its provisions. Because the legislation would establish a new offense, the government would be able to pursue cases that it otherwise would not be…

earliest_articles.title: S. 1324, the Captive Primate Safety Act
earliest_articles.excerpt: S. 1324 would amend the Lacey Act to prohibit interstate and foreign trade of nonhuman primates. The legislation also would authorize the appropriation of $3 million a year over the 2013-2015 period for the U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service (USFWS) to carry out that act. 1324 would cost $9 million over the 2013-2015…

earliest_articles.title: H.R. 4100, Illegal, Unreported, and Unregulated Fishing Enforcement Act of 2011
earliest_articles.excerpt: As ordered reported by the House Committee on Natural Resources on July 11, 2012 H.R. 4100 would strengthen the authority of the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) to enforce fisheries laws and combat illegal, unreported, and unregulated (IUU) fishing by foreign vessels. The bill would establish…

newest_articles.title: S. 2357, Young Fishermen’s Development Program Reauthorization Act
newest_articles.excerpt: S. 2357 would reauthorize the appropriation of $2 million each year from 2027 through 2031 for the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) to provide grants for training, education, outreach, and technical assistance to young fishermen. In 2024, the Congress appropriated $1 million for the program. The…

newest_articles.title: H.R. 3692, a bill to reauthorize the Young Fishermen’s Development Act
newest_articles.excerpt: H.R. 3692 would reauthorize the appropriation of $2 million each year from 2027 through 2031 for the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration to provide grants for training, education, outreach, and technical assistance to young fishermen. newest_articles.title: S. 688, FISH Act of 2025
newest_articles.excerpt: As ordered reported by the Senate Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation on April 30, 2025 On This Page Bill Summary S. 688 would reauthorize the National Sea Grant College Program until September 30, 2031. In addition, the bill would authorize appropriations for the National Oceanic and Atmospheric…

central_articles.title: H.R. 737, Shark Fin Sales Elimination Act of 2019
central_articles.excerpt: H.R. 737 would prohibit the sale, possession, and purchase of shark fins and products containing shark fins. Violators of this prohibition would be subject to a civil penalty pursuant to the Magnuson-Stevens Fishery Conservation and Management Act. The bill also would allow for the lawful possession of shark fins…
